agent_notes: Barnet did not support the 30-35 target without padding: no PAINTS-approved Creative Walk route sits inside the borough, and true Books & Coffee venues are especially sparse after Black Gull Books closed.
Several venues have strong cultural or neighbourhood fit but weaker live operational evidence for walk-ins/groups of 8-12; these are marked for host confirmation.
Museum/gallery supply is civic and local rather than press-led; current-exhibition validation is strongest for artsdepot and weakest for Barnet Museum/Stephens Collection.

venues.neighbourhood_station: East Finchley / Highgate, East Finchley tube
venues.opening_hours: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Instagram and press confirm active venue/address; live daily hours not reliably published in indexed sources.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm group walk-in. Bottle-shop/wine-bar model appears walk-in friendly but 8-12 seating must be checked.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable only if seated in courtyard/showroom-adjacent area; host must call ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Wine shop/deli in Hexagon Classics car showroom setting; exact seated group capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 17:30-19:30 or Sunday early afternoon, avoiding peak dinner spillover.
venues.why_fits_paints: A wine shop embedded in a classic car showroom is exactly the sort of slightly odd North London texture PAINTS should reward. It has a sommelier-led selection story and enough visual strangeness to give strangers something immediate to talk about. Operationally, it needs a host call because public walk-in/group details are thin.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened as an indie wine/deli concept inside Hexagon Classics; Time Out reported it was from sommelier Bert Blaize, co-author of Which Wine When.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine, deli goods, artisan coffee references, and a wine-shop/car-showroom setting.
venues.safety_notes: Great North Road is a high-footfall corridor near East Finchley station; late-evening walk to station is straightforward but host should keep the group on main roads.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup is an Urban Pubs & Bars site, so it is less independent/PAINTS-coded; use only operationally. Primary hours and group seating are the binding caveat.

venues.opening_hours: Open seven days; London Brewing lists Mon-Thu 16:00-23:00, Fri 16:00-01:00, Sat 12:00-01:00, Sun 12:00-22:30. Host must confirm current July 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm group walk-in. Pub format suggests walk-ins, but 8-12 should call ahead.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good: large independent brewpub with basement event area and regular pub seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Tagvenue lists Bar Below capacity up to 60 seated / 100 standing, useful as evidence of group tolerance though private-hire terms differ from walk-in use.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper North Finchley brewpub with house-brewed beer gives the event a making-process story, not just a drinks list. It is relaxed, unshowy, and has enough local credibility for people to feel they discovered something. It is less wine-led than ideal, but the brewery angle compensates.
venues.anecdote_story: Home of London Brewing Company; CAMRA notes brewery-linked activity including meet-the-brewer socials and cask/keg offers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: House-brewed craft beer, cask ales, pub food, regular events.
venues.safety_notes: High Road/Woodside Park route is urban and well-lit; avoid sending attendees through quiet side streets late at night.
venues.risks_watchouts: Primary is beer-led rather than natural-wine-led; backup is Fuller's and sports-screen heavy, so only a practical fallback.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists open hours beginning Monday 12:00; CAMRA lists daily opening with later weekend closes. Host must confirm exact day/date.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm 8-12 walk-in. Traditional pub format, but small hidden-away pub may need a heads-up.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible but not guaranteed; better for 8 than 12 unless seated early.
venues.capacity_notes: Small, cosy traditional pub; no TVs/gimmicks per official/Punch listing.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the anti-generic High Barnet pub: tucked away, local, and story-bearing. The CAMRA and local press trail gives it more cultural weight than a normal pint stop. It stays Tier B because group walk-in certainty is weak.
venues.anecdote_story: Barnet Society reports the pub was a CAMRA Pub of the Year runner-up and had former film-star customers including Richard Burton and Elizabeth Taylor.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Traditional pub drinks and home-cooked food.
venues.safety_notes: Short walk from High Barnet High Street but quieter residential lane; host should direct attendees as a group back to the main road/tube after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 13 min walk - outside strict 10-min backup rule
venues.risks_watchouts: No qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min; closest PAINTS-coded alternative is Ye Olde Monken Holt at about 13 min, so primary remains Tier B.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Hadley / High Barnet, High Barnet tube
venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists Fri/Sat noon-midnight, Sun noon-23:00, Mon noon-23:00, Tue-Thu noon-midnight; host must confirm current July 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm group walk-in. Pub appears walk-in oriented.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable at off-peak; sports/pub-quiz nights may make conversation harder.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional family pub with real ale and regular pub events.
venues.why_fits_paints: A no-frills Hadley pub gives Barnet list geographic reach beyond Finchley and Colindale. The fit is character and locality rather than design polish. It clears the floor but not Tier A because press/story depth is modest.
venues.anecdote_story: Layers of London records a pub on the site since 1869; CAMRA identifies the current venue as an independent pub.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ale/pub drinks, pub food, quiz/live sport setting.
venues.safety_notes: High Street route is direct; late-night attendees should be guided toward High Barnet station by main roads.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup is hotel-adjacent and therefore only a pragmatic fallback; primary has limited cultural press.

venues.opening_hours: Official/Friends pages and Instagram indicate Wednesday and Saturday market trading around 8:00-15:00; host must confirm trader turnout for target date.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a loose market format; meet at a cafe first and split into pairs for browsing.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor town market; trader mix varies by day/weather.
venues.why_fits_paints: The market has the rare thing Barnet does better than trendier boroughs: deep continuity. It is not slick, but the charter history gives a host a real opening story. The PAINTS version should be framed as a local-history market wander rather than a gourmet market.
venues.anecdote_story: Barnet Council traces the market to at least the 12th century, with King John's 1199 charter and a later Elizabeth I charter in 1588.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Produce, fishmonger, food/drink and rotating local stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime High Barnet setting beside shops and car parks; standard town-centre caution.
venues.backup_venue: North Finchley Market, Lodge Lane Car Park
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by bus/tube-linked local transit - permitted for market backup
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and trader turnout are the main risks; meeting point Coffee Bean Barnet is a chain, so host should verify an independent cafe alternative if possible.

venues.venue_name: North Finchley Market, Lodge Lane Car Park
venues.opening_hours: Facebook market page says every Friday 8:00-15:00; planning documents confirm part of Lodge Lane car park is used for a weekly market. Host must confirm current trader list.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a short, casual market stop, not a premium destination.
venues.capacity_notes: Car-park market with redevelopment pressure; atmosphere likely varies strongly by trader mix.
venues.why_fits_paints: This clears only as an operational Barnet backup: local, open-air, and close to artsdepot/Bohemia. It is not visually special enough for Tier A. Use it when the point is neighbourhood texture rather than a destination market.
venues.anecdote_story: Lodge Lane has a layered local-history footnote: Barnet Council notes the lane was home to John Parr, the first British soldier killed in World War I.
venues.serve_or_on_view: General market stalls; food offer must be checked on the day.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime town-centre setting; redevelopment works around Lodge Lane may affect routes and ambience.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by local transit - permitted for market backup
venues.risks_watchouts: Binding risk is quality/curation, not access. Do not frame as artisan unless current traders prove it.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: open daily 10:00-17:00, last entry 16:30; free admission, booking recommended.
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed/free ticketing recommended; walk-in admission may be possible but host must pre-book group to avoid fragility.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good; large museum campus supports groups, but book free tickets in advance.
venues.capacity_notes: Large hangars, cafe, and free-entry museum campus.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Barnet's strongest museum anchor: huge, cinematic, and rooted in the former Hendon Aerodrome. It works for PAINTS if hosted through design, engineering, propaganda, and material culture rather than a generic family day out. The hangars give strangers shared objects to react to immediately.
venues.anecdote_story: The London site occupies the former RAF Hendon aerodrome and opened as the museum in 1972.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Aircraft, aviation artefacts, RAF history displays, hangar collections.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime museum campus; route from Colindale involves ordinary suburban roads, best done in daylight or with clear directions.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - closest cultural alternative is artsdepot, about 20+ min by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight same-category backup within PAINTS radius, so demoted to Tier B despite strong museum fit; host should pre-book free tickets.

venues.opening_hours: Current homepage/what's-on pages list building/cafe/box office hours and Community Focus Summer Exhibition 2026, Mon 29 Jun-Fri 24 Jul 2026 at 10:00; host must confirm gallery access on chosen day.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gallery/exhibition appears free/drop-in; ticketed performances separate. Host must confirm group walk-in access.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a hosted exhibition wall visit plus cafe discussion.
venues.capacity_notes: Arts centre with gallery/exhibition wall, cafe, theatre and studio spaces.
venues.why_fits_paints: artsdepot is the most flexible visual-culture venue in Barnet, with current exhibition programming and an on-site cafe. The Community Focus show is locally grounded, which suits PAINTS better than a generic blockbuster. It is not a blue-chip gallery, but it is operationally useful and genuinely borough-specific.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue opened in 2004 as a North Finchley multi-arts centre; local listings describe it as Barnet's award-winning arts centre.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Community Focus Summer Exhibition 2026 on the Exhibition Wall, plus wider theatre/music/community arts programme.
venues.safety_notes: Town-centre site by bus station and High Road; use main routes after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk - cultural drinks fallback, not same-category gallery
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup is not same category; if the exhibition wall is unavailable, this should become a drinks event rather than a museum/gallery day.

venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-in during public hours appears accepted; groups/out-of-hours visits can be arranged, so host must confirm 8-12.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a small local-history group if museum volunteers approve.
venues.capacity_notes: Small volunteer-run museum; group movement may be tight.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a small museum with unusually strong local story density: Battle of Barnet, coaching inns, domestic rooms, and local business objects. It is less visually polished than PAINTS ideal, but a good host could turn it into an intimate civic-history session. Tier B because opening hours are narrow and current exhibition press is limited.
venues.anecdote_story: Visit London lists Battle of Barnet material, Victorian rooms, costume, pub/coaching-trade material and local history collections.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local history artefacts, Battle of Barnet material, costume, domestic and Victorian-room displays.
venues.safety_notes: Central High Barnet location; daytime only is preferable because museum hours are mostly daytime.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk - market/civic-history fallback, not same-category museum
venues.risks_watchouts: Narrow volunteer opening window; no recent press-led current exhibition found.

venues.venue_name: Stephens House & Gardens / The Stephens Collection
venues.neighbourhood_station: Finchley Church End, Finchley Central tube
venues.opening_hours: Gardens daily 08:00-18:00 summer; cafe 09:00-17:00 weekdays and 10:00 weekends/bank holidays. Stephens Collection is by appointment.
venues.walk_in_policy: Gardens/cafe walk-in; Stephens Collection and house access by appointment/guided tour only - Tier B operational cap.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for gardens/cafe; collection viewing requires prior arrangement.
venues.capacity_notes: Estate supports groups outdoors; indoor heritage/collection access is controlled.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 10:00-12:00 if Bothy Gardens are open, or weekday 14:00-16:00 with pre-arranged collection access.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Inky Stephens story is lovely: ink, philanthropy, house, gardens, and a local collection. It feels personal and borough-specific, with enough material culture to spark conversation. It cannot be Tier A because the collection is not reliably walk-in.
venues.anecdote_story: Henry 'Inky' Stephens left the estate for public enjoyment; the Stephens Collection documents the famous blue-black writing fluid and the Stephens family/company.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Stephens ink/company artefacts by appointment, gardens, Bothy Gardens, Inky's Cafe, Spike Milligan statue on the grounds.
venues.safety_notes: Daylight garden/cafe visit recommended; do not treat the gardens as Creative Walks because Stephens House is not on the PAINTS approved park list.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk - currently rejected as primary due closure/conflicting signals
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup is not reliable because CAMRA/local sources report Catcher in the Rye closure/lease uncertainty despite official pages; host must identify a live fallback before publishing.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Hampstead Heath Extension / Golders Hill Park routes
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list. The brief explicitly says parks not on the list must be treated as excluded, even if famous or attractive.
Atmospheric and locally historic, but not on the PAINTS-approved Creative Walk safety list. Could be reviewed by the PAINTS team later, but cannot be sourced now.
PAINTS-approved Safety-First park, but it is in Enfield/Cockfosters rather than Barnet for this district output.
Both are PAINTS-approved Safety-First parks but sit in Haringey, not Barnet; do not use to pad Barnet.
Plausible PAINTS fit historically, but closed permanently/closure reported; cannot be sourced as a live venue.
Bookshop is live, but it is a chain and no qualifying independent coffee/bookshop-cafe offer was verified; PAINTS v6 rejects chains and pure bookshops without coffee partner.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: GET Free Books, The Spires High Barnet
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Community bookshop idea is warm, but it has relocation/temporary-premises uncertainty and does not verify a substantive on-site independent coffee offer; also inside The Spires shopping centre context.
Good small-family-run cafe signal, but no substantive books/bookshop/zine/library offer found, so it fails the raised Books & Coffee rule.

Conflicting live status: official pages/Instagram suggest activity, but CAMRA and local newsletter sources report temporary closure/lease uncertainty around late 2025. Too operationally fragile until host confirms reopened.
Excellent historic coaching-inn story, but currently a Greene King pub; PAINTS v6 says no chains for Tier A and the chain status makes it unsuitable as a primary PAINTS pick.
Historic 300-year-old pub story is strong, but 2025 sources report closure/operator uncertainty and Instagram signals refurbishment/pop-up status; too fragile for inclusion.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Museum of Domestic Design and Architecture (MoDA)
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Strong design/craft fit historically, but MoDA is now closed and collections were dispersed; cannot be a live venue.

Wonderful independent cinema and cultural backup, but it does not reliably meet the museum/gallery rule requiring paintings, sculpture, textiles, ceramics or craft with a current exhibition; no current PAINTS-qualifying exhibition found.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Courtyard Market N2 / Engine Rooms artisan market
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Very PAINTS-coded seasonal artisan market, but the sourced pages are date-specific/seasonal rather than a reliable current recurring market for July 2026.
London Farmers Markets page states the market is no longer open.
